name: Mr. Zhang
initialComplaint: I have been feeling a bit short of breath lately, especially when I walk to the market.

hiddenDetails.duration: It started about 3 months ago and has been getting worse.
hiddenDetails.triggers: Mainly physical exertion. It gets better when I sit down for a few minutes.
hiddenDetails.pastHistory: I have high blood pressure, but I sometimes forget to take my pills.
hiddenDetails.associatedSymptoms: My ankles have been looking a bit swollen in the evenings.
hiddenDetails.lifestyle: I smoked for 30 years but quit 5 years ago. I don't exercise much.
hiddenDetails.familyHistory: My father had a heart attack at 58. My mother had diabetes.
hiddenDetails.drugHistory: I take amlodipine for blood pressure but often forget. No other medications.
hiddenDetails.reviewOfSystems: Some occasional chest tightness on exertion. No palpitations. Needing two pillows to sleep comfortably lately.

physicalExam.finding: Displaced apex beat, S3 gallop heard on auscultation.
Fine crackles at both lung bases.

mustAskItems.hint: When did the shortness of breath start?
Does anything help with the breathing?
How do you sleep at night? Do you use extra pillows?
Do you have any medical conditions?
What medications do you take? Do you take them regularly?
Does anyone in your family have heart problems?
How active are you day to day?
Do you ever get chest pain?
Do you ever feel your heart racing?
What are you most worried about?

personality: Stoic elderly man, downplays symptoms. Reluctant to admit he forgets medication. Speaks slowly and deliberately.
